title: designRequirements
description: The requirements an analog IC must comply with. Built on the PEAS designRequirementsBase mixin, with a deviceType discriminator that aligns with AAS's top-level family discriminator, plus a small set of generic selection seeds. Unlike SAS, AAS keeps a single generic seed rather than an 11-branch per-family oneOf: analog-IC selection seeds are looser (a designer usually filters on family + a couple of headline specs), so a per-family required-ratings oneOf would add fields without adding coverage. Family-specific required ratings, if ever needed, can be added later as a deviceType-discriminated oneOf.

deviceType.description: Required analog-IC family. Aligns with AAS's top-level family discriminator.

minimumChannels.description: Minimum number of channels the part must provide.

supplyVoltage.description: Total supply voltage available to the part, in V.

maximumQuiescentCurrent.description: Maximum acceptable quiescent current per channel, in A.

maximumInputOffsetVoltage.description: Maximum acceptable input offset voltage, in V (precision amplifier seeds).

minimumBandwidth.description: Minimum required signal bandwidth / GBW, in Hz (amplifier seeds).

minimumResolution.description: Minimum required resolution, in bits (converter seeds).
